description_long: This fire hydrant features a robust, cylindrical body with a domed top and a metallic red finish, accented by subtle weathering and darker patches that suggest exposure to the elements. The hydrant has three prominent hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one facing forward, each capped and slightly protruding. A central operating nut is visible on the top, designed for wrench operation to open the valve. The base is octagonal, providing stability and a classic urban aesthetic, while the body is vertically ribbed for structural strength. The surface is glossy and reflective, with highlights and shadows accentuating its metallic texture. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant is shown isolated without any curb or sidewalk context.
description: A glossy red, weathered fire hydrant with three capped outlets and a domed top, featuring an octagonal base and metallic sheen.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from a slight angle, highlighting the front outlet and the right-side outlet, with the domed top and octagonal base clearly visible.
description_view_1: A direct front view showing all three hose outlets symmetrically, with the central operating nut and the full width of the hydrant's body.
description_view_2: The left-side outlet is most prominent, with the front outlet partially visible and the domed top and base in clear view.
description_view_3: A rear view showing the back of the hydrant, with both side outlets visible but the front outlet obscured, emphasizing the ribbed body and base.
